name: Bicep curl
description:
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward.
Slowly raise both dumbbells at the same time by bending your elbows, while keeping your upper arms stationary. Keep your palms facing forward throughout the movement.
Continue to curl the dumbbells until they are level with your shoulders or until you feel a good contraction in your biceps.
Pause for a second at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for your desired number of reps.

name: Lat pull down
description:
Start by attaching a wide handle bar to the cable.
Sit on the lat pulldown machine with your feet flat on the ground and your knees firmly pressed against the pad. Your thighs should be parallel to the ground.
Grab the bar with both hands, making sure your palms are facing away from you and your grip is wider than shoulder-width apart.
While keeping your back straight and your chest up, pull the bar down towards your chest by contracting your back muscles. Your elbows should point downwards and stay close to your body.
Pause for a second when the bar reaches your chest, and then slowly release it back up to the starting position, extending your arms fully.
Repeat for the desired number of reps.

name: Seated cable row
description:
Place your feet firmly on the footrest or platform provided by the cable machine.
Keep your back straight and avoid slouching or rounding your shoulders forward. Engage your core muscles to help maintain good posture. This will help you maintain stability and prevent your lower back from rounding.
Before starting the exercise, retract your shoulder blades and keep them in this position throughout the movement. This will help engage the muscles of the upper back and prevent excessive strain on the shoulders and neck.
As you pull the cable handles towards you, keep your elbows flared out to the sides and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement. This will help engage the muscles of the upper back and prevent excessive strain on the shoulders.
Pause for a second at the top of the movement and then slowly release back to the starting position. Exhale as you pull the cable towards your body and inhale as you return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Face pulls
description:
Set Up: Attach a rope handle to a cable machine or use resistance bands at chest or eye level.
Grip: Hold the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Position: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart, engaging your core.
Execution: Pull the rope toward your face, elbows flaring outward. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the peak of the movement. Slowly return to the starting position.

name: Cable crossover
description:
Adjust the pulleys to the desired height. High Pulley: Targets the lower chest. Low Pulley: Engages the upper chest. Mid Pulley: Works the middle chest. Attach D-handles to each pulley.
Stand in the center of the machine with one foot slightly forward for balance.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing each other and step forward slightly to create tension on the cables.
Start with your arms outstretched to the sides, elbows slightly bent.
Pull the handles downward and together in a wide arc, as if hugging a barrel.
Squeeze your chest at the peak of the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position, maintaining control.

name: Low cable crossover
description:
Start by setting the cable machine to the lowest setting and attaching a handle to each side.
Stand in the center of the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and facing the machine. Grab a handle in each hand and bring your hands up to shoulder height. This is the starting position.
Take a step forward with one foot, keeping your knees slightly bent, and lean forward at the waist while maintaining a straight back.
Your hands should end up at your sides, just below your hips.
With a slight bend in your arms, draw your weights upward and toward the midline of your body. Make sure to keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.

name: Overhead press
description:
Starting Position: Grasp a barbell or body bar with an overhand grip. Your hands should be wider than shoulder width.
Raise the barbell so that it is just below your chin, and keep your knees slightly bent.
While keeping your core muscles engaged, exhale and press your arms over your head. Hold for a brief second at the top and then slowly lower back to the starting position.
You can perform this exercise with your feet parallel or with your feet staggered. When your feet are parallel there is more challenge on balance and there is also more challenge on your lower back.
If you want to take some pressure off your lower back use a staggered stance.
As with all exercises, posture is very important. Make sure that your back does not arch excessively when you press over head.
It is important to note that proper form and technique are crucial when performing the barbell military press to avoid injury. It is recommended to start with lighter weights and gradually increase the load as your strength and technique improve.

name: Tricep pushdown
description:
Attach a rope handle to a high pulley cable machine.
Select an appropriate weight that allows for controlled movement.
Stand facing the machine with a slight forward lean, keeping your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with both hands, using a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Keep your elbows close to your torso, and engage your core.
Push the rope down by extending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
As you reach the bottom, separate the rope handles, emphasizing a full contraction of the triceps.
Hold the contraction for a second, then slowly return to the starting position while controlling the resistance.
Repeat for 3 to 4 sets of 10 to 12 reps with proper form.

name: Cable lateral raise
description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Extend your arms out to the sides, parallel to the floor, with your palms facing down.
Keeping your arms straight, lift the weights up and out to the sides until they reach shoulder height.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the weights back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Supinated Cable Chest Press
description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your back straight.
Attach the cable to the pulley at chest height.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Step forward slightly to create tension in the cable.
Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended in front of you.
Hold for a second, then slowly release back to the starting position.
Repeat for desired number of reps.

name: Push Up
description:
Begin in a high plank position with hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core and keep your neck neutral.
Inhale as you bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the ground.
Keep your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso.
Lower until your chest is just above the floor or as far as mobility allows.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to the starting position.
Fully extend your arms while maintaining core engagement.
Perform controlled reps at a steady pace to maximize muscle engagement.

name: Barbell Squat
description:
Set the barbell on a squat rack just below shoulder height. Step under it and rest the bar across your upper traps or rear delts.
Grip the bar firmly, pull your elbows down and under, and lift the bar off the rack.
Step back, set your feet shoulder-width apart, and brace your core.
Lower your body by pushing your hips back and bending the knees, keeping your chest upright.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the ground or lower if mobility allows.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position, keeping the spine neutral..

name: Static Lunge
description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
Take a step forward with one foot, and then take a larger step backward with your other foot. Your feet should be positioned so that when you lower your body into the lunge, both knees form 90-degree angles.
Keep your torso upright with your chest lifted and your shoulders relaxed. Your core should be engaged for stability.
Lower your body by bending both knees simultaneously.
Hold the lowered position for a moment, and then push through the heel of your front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions on one leg before switching to the other leg.

name: Cable Romanian Deadlift
description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a cable attachment at the lowest setting on a cable machine.
Grab the cable attachment with both hands and step back until there is tension on the cable.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and lower the cable attachment towards the ground.
Lower the cable attachment as far as you can while maintaining proper form and feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Slowly raise the cable attachment back up to the starting position, squeezing your glutes and hamstrings at the top of the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of reps.
target_area: Hamstrings, Glutes, Erector spinae, Core muscles

name: Hip Adduction
description:
Adjust the machine: Before starting, adjust the machine to your height and select the appropriate weight. Make sure the pads are positioned comfortably on your legs and your feet are firmly on the footrests.
Sit on the machine: Sit on the seat with your back against the backrest and your feet on the footrests.
Position your legs: Position your legs so that your knees are bent at a 90-degree angle and your thighs are slightly apart.
Engage your inner thighs: Squeeze your inner thighs together and push your legs against the resistance. Exhale as you bring your legs together.
Release: Slowly release the resistance and allow your legs to return to the starting position. Inhale as you release.
Repeat: Complete the desired number of repetitions and sets, and adjust the weight as needed.

name: Hip Abduction
description:
Start by sitting on the lever seated hip abduction machine with your back straight and your feet flat on the footrests.
Adjust the machine so that the pads are resting just above your knees.
Place your hands on the handles provided on either side of the seat.
Slowly push your legs outwards, away from each other, until you feel a stretch in your hip muscles.
Hold this position for a few seconds, then slowly bring your legs back together.
Repeat this movement for the desired number of repetitions.
Remember to keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Once you have completed your set, carefully stand up and step off the machine.

name: Seated Leg Curl
description:
Set the leg curl machine to your appropriate height and adjust the resistance level according to your fitness level and goals.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your legs extended in front of you. Make sure your knees are aligned with the pivot point of the machine.
Place the back of your lower legs (just above the heels) under the padded lever or roller of the machine. The pad should rest comfortably against your legs, and your knees should be slightly off the edge of the seat.
Grasp the handles provided on the machine for stability and support throughout the exercise. Keep your upper body relaxed and maintain good posture.
Bend your knees, bringing your heels toward your buttocks while maintaining control and avoiding any jerking or swinging motions.
Continue the movement until your legs are fully bent and the machine pad is close to your glutes.
Pause briefly in the contracted position, focusing on squeezing your hamstrings.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight by extending your legs back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the descent.
Repeat the exercise for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Battle Rope
description:
Anchor the ropes securely and stand facing the anchor with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold one rope end in each hand, bend your knees slightly, and brace your core.
Hinge at the hips into an athletic stance with your chest up and back neutral.
Raise and lower your arms quickly to create alternating waves through the ropes.
Keep your torso stable while breathing steadily and maintaining a fast, controlled rhythm.
Continue for the target time, then slow down gradually and stand tall to finish.

name: Cobra Pose
description:
Start by lying face-down on a mat with your hands placed flat under your shoulders.
Slowly press into your hands and straighten your arms, lifting your chest and head off the ground.
Keep your elbows close to your body and hold this position for a few seconds, breathing deeply.
Then, release back down to the starting position. Repeat several times.
It is important to perform the cobra pose carefully and gradually, avoiding any sudden movements that could cause pain or injury.
If you experience discomfort during the stretch, stop and speak with a healthcare professional.
Additionally, it is important to incorporate regular stretching into your overall exercise program to maintain flexibility and prevent injury.

name: Cross Body Mountain Climber
description:
Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core muscles by drawing your navel toward your spine and maintaining a stable core throughout the exercise.
Lift your right foot off the ground and bring your right knee toward your left elbow, crossing your body diagonally.
As you bring your knee in, engage your core and maintain a strong plank position.
Extend your right leg back to the starting position while simultaneously repeating the movement on the other side.
Lift your left foot off the ground and bring your left knee toward your right elbow, crossing your body diagonally.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or follow a set time frame for your workout.

name: Crunch
description:
Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands lightly behind your head or across your chest.
Press your lower back gently into the floor and engage your abdominal muscles.
Exhale and lift your head, shoulders, and upper back off the floor using your abs.
Keep your neck neutral and avoid pulling on your head with your hands.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower slowly with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping each rep smooth and controlled.

name: Heel Touch
description:
Lie on your back with your arms by your sides, palms down, and bend your knees. Engage your core to raise your shoulders and upper back off the floor, keeping your neck relaxed and your spine in a neutral position.
Reach down as far as you can toward your right foot with your right hand in a slow and controlled movement, crunching the left side of your abdomen to drive the movement and avoid straining with your neck.
Pause briefly, then return to the starting position and alternate for the required number of repetitions. Repeat the movement to the left side of your body.

name: High Plank
description:
Start in a push-up position, with hands directly under the shoulders and legs extended behind you.
Engage your core, squeeze your glutes, and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Press firmly through the hands, keeping your shoulders active and stable.
Hold the position while breathing steadily, avoiding any sagging or arching.
Begin with 20–30 seconds, gradually increasing time as strength improves.

name: Jump Rope
description:
Choose a rope length that reaches your armpits when stood on with both feet.
Hold the handles at hip height with elbows bent and wrists slightly forward.
Stand tall with your feet together, core tight, and knees slightly bent.
Begin turning the rope with your wrists while keeping your shoulders relaxed.
Jump just high enough to clear the rope, landing softly on the balls of your feet.
Maintain a steady rhythm, keeping your jumps small and controlled.

name: Kettlebell Swings
description:
Stand with your feet shoulder width apart and grip the kettlebell with both hands, allowing it to hang loosely. Bend your knees and drop your buttocks backward slightly, leaning your torso forward from the hip and keeping your back straight.
Keeping your arms straight but relaxed, drive forward with your hips to stand tall, so that the kettlebell swings forward and up.
Allow the kettlebell to swing up as far as momentum carries it, and then back down toward the hips. As it approaches, drop your hips and lean your torso forwards, and keeping your back straight, to return to the start position.

name: Leg Raise
description:
Lie on your back with your arms placed by your sides. Raise your legs into a vertical position, keeping your knees and feet together and your back and hips neutral.
Slowly lower your legs, keeping your feet and knees together, controlling the movement with your core and keeping your torso stationary.
Continue the movement until your feet are as near to the floor as you can get, maintaining a neutral back. Hold this position briefly, then slowly lift your legs back to the start position, with a controlled, smooth motion. Avoid lifting your lower back as you repeat.

name: Reverse Crunch
description:
Lie on your back with your arms by your sides and bend your knees to about 90 degrees.
Engage your core and flatten your lower back gently against the floor.
Exhale and curl your pelvis upward, bringing your knees toward your chest.
Lift only until your hips slightly leave the floor; avoid swinging your legs.
Inhale and lower your hips back down slowly to the start position.
Repeat for controlled reps while keeping tension in your abs throughout.

name: Rowing Machine
description:
Start Position (The Catch): Sit on the seat with feet strapped in. Bend your knees, lean slightly forward at the hips, and hold the handle with an overhand grip.
Drive Phase: Push explosively through the legs, extend hips, then pull the handle toward your upper abdomen while keeping elbows close to the body
Finish Position: Legs fully extended, torso leaning slightly back, handle pulled just below the chest
Recovery Phase: Extend arms forward first, then hinge at the hips, and finally bend knees to return to the catch
Maintain a steady rhythm, coordinating legs, core, and arms smoothly.

name: Running
description:
Start with a brief warm-up walk or light jog for 3 to 5 minutes.
Stand tall with your head neutral, shoulders relaxed, and core engaged.
Begin running at a manageable pace with short, quick steps and soft landings.
Keep your arms bent around 90 degrees and swing them naturally front to back.
Breathe rhythmically and maintain a steady pace you can control.
Finish with a slow jog or walk for cooldown and light lower-body stretching.

name: Scissor Kick
description:
Lie on your back with your legs extended and hands under your hips for support if needed.
Engage your core and lift both legs a few inches off the floor.
Raise one leg upward while the other leg stays low, keeping knees mostly straight.
Switch leg positions in a controlled, alternating scissor motion.
Keep your lower back pressed toward the floor and avoid arching.
Continue for the target reps or time, then lower both legs slowly to finish.

name: Side Bridge
description:
Lie on your side with your forearm on the floor.
Position the elbow directly under the shoulder.
Stack your legs straight on top of each other, feet aligned.
Keep the body in a straight line from head to heels.
Slowly lower your hips toward the floor under control.
Maintain shoulder alignment and avoid collapsing into the elbow.
Engage the obliques to lift the hips back up.
Return to a straight body position without overextending.
Repeat the controlled lowering and lifting motion for the desired number of repetitions.
Keep the movement smooth and stable.
Complete all repetitions on one side before changing sides.

name: Side Plank
description:
Lie on your right side, raising yourself onto your right forearm.
Place your right shoulder directly above your right elbow, keeping your shoulders, hips and knees in one straight line.
Rest your left arm along the side of your body.
Before you press up, draw your abs in, drop your shoulders away from your ears, and lengthen your spine.
Push up so that you body is straight… Hold for 10 seconds for beginner to 1 Minute if more advanced. Repeat on your left side.

name: Stair Stepper
description:
Step onto the machine and place both feet fully on the pedals.
Hold the handles lightly for balance, keeping your chest up and core tight.
Start stepping at a comfortable pace with smooth, even pressure through each leg.
Keep your posture upright and avoid leaning heavily on the handles.
Increase resistance or pace gradually as your heart rate rises.
End with 2 to 3 minutes at an easier pace before stepping off carefully.

name: Standing Cable Twist
description:
Set a cable handle at chest height and stand sideways to the machine.
Hold the handle with both hands, arms extended in front of your chest.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Brace your core and rotate your torso away from the machine in a controlled motion.
Pause briefly at end range, then return slowly to the start without letting the stack slam.
Complete all reps on one side, then switch sides and repeat.

name: Standing Rotation
description:
Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and hold your hands together in front of your chest.
Keep your knees soft and your core engaged to stabilize your spine.
Rotate your torso to one side while keeping hips mostly facing forward.
Return to center with control, then rotate to the opposite side.
Move through a pain-free range and avoid forcing the rotation.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Hyperextension
description:
Position your thighs on the pads of the Roman chair so that your hips are free to flex. Your feet should be flat on the foot supports, your spine neutral, and your elbows pointing out.
Flex at your hips and drop your upper body toward the floor, keeping your back flat. Stop bending when the flexibility of your hamstrings restricts further movement.
Return to the start position, contracting your hamstrings, glutes, and spinal erectors. Do not extend beyond the start position because you may injure your back

name: Stationary Bike
description:
Adjust the seat so your knee has a slight bend at the bottom of each pedal stroke.
Place your feet securely on the pedals and set a light starting resistance.
Begin pedaling smoothly while keeping your chest up and shoulders relaxed.
Maintain even cadence and breathe steadily as you settle into pace.
Increase resistance gradually based on your workout goal.
Cool down at low resistance for a few minutes before stopping.

name: Treadmill Run
description:
Set the treadmill to a slow walk for warm-up, then progress to an easy jog.
Increase speed to your target running pace once you feel ready.
Run with a slight forward lean from the ankles, relaxed shoulders, and engaged core.
Keep your stride controlled and avoid overstriding in front of your body.
Use handrails only for balance when needed, not for continuous support.
Reduce speed gradually at the end and walk to cool down.

name: Treadmill
description:
Step onto the side rails, start the belt at a slow speed, then begin walking.
Stand upright with your eyes forward and arms swinging naturally.
Set a comfortable pace and optional incline based on your workout goal.
Keep your steps consistent and avoid holding the rails unless necessary.
Monitor effort and breathing, adjusting speed as needed.
Finish by lowering speed gradually and walking for cooldown before stepping off.

name: Walking
description:
Stand tall with shoulders relaxed, chest open, and eyes looking forward.
Begin walking at a comfortable pace with natural arm swing.
Land softly from heel to toe while keeping your stride smooth and even.
Keep your core lightly engaged and maintain steady breathing.
Increase pace gradually if you want higher intensity.
Slow down for the last few minutes to cool down before stopping.
